name: Sierra de Navalmanzano by the Pinturas Rupestres Peña Escrita — circular from Fuencaliente
name_original: Sierra de Navalmanzano por las Pinturas Rupestres Peña Escrita — circular desde Fuencaliente
description: This difficult 14.5-mile (23.3 km) jogging route from Fuencaliente features ancient rock paintings at Peña Escrita and 1957 feet of elevatio
region_intro: Jogging around Fuencaliente offers a unique experience through its dramatic volcanic landscapes and diverse natural features. The region is characterized by lava fields, dark peaks, and trails winding through volcanic cones, providing varied and inspiring running routes. Runners can explore coastal areas with black sand beaches and salt pans, as well as vineyards clinging to volcanic slopes. This varied terrain ensures a dynamic environment for all types of running. Best jogging routes around Fuencaliente The most popular jogging route is Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) moderate trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the Batanera waterfall area and ancient rock paintings. Another top favourite among local runners is Ascent to Los Rehoyos (Sierra de la Solana) — circular from Solana del Pino, a difficult 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de la Solana. Local runners also love the Ascent to Serra Madrona — circular from Solana del Pino, a 8.9 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Jogging around Fuencaliente is defined by volcanic terrain, coastal scenery, and vineyards. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Fuencaliente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Fuencaliente?
A: Fuencaliente offers a diverse network of over 10 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. While there is one moderate route, the majority, 9 of them, are considered difficult, providing challenging experiences through the region's unique volcanic landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Fuencaliente?
A: Running in Fuencaliente means encountering dramatic and varied terrain. You'll find trails winding through volcanic cones and lava fields, coastal paths with breathtaking Atlantic views, and routes through vineyards clinging to volcanic slopes. Many paths feature volcanic gravel (picón), so good footwear is recommended.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Fuencaliente?
A: While Fuencaliente is known for its more challenging routes, there is one moderate running trail available: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt;. This 4.9-mile (7.9 km) route offers a good introduction to the area's beauty without extreme difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Fuencaliente?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Fuencaliente are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt;, and more challenging options like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304437503/ascent-to-los-rehoyos-sierra-de-la-solana-circular-from-solana-del-pino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Los Rehoyos (Sierra de la Solana) — circular from Solana del Pino&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by waterfalls or historical sites?
A: Absolutely! You can combine your run with visits to natural and historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, explores the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; area and ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3169663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescrita Cave Paintings&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lastras&lt;/a&gt; also feature waterfalls.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while running in Fuencaliente?
A: Fuencaliente is rich in natural beauty. Beyond the waterfalls, you can explore dramatic volcanic landscapes, including the San Antonio Volcano and Teneguía Volcano, which offer stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lastras&lt;/a&gt; are prominent natural features. The Salinas de Fuencaliente (Salt Pans) also provide a unique coastal and volcanic setting.

Q: Are there any unique cultural or historical sites along the running paths?
A: Yes, you can discover fascinating historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3169663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescrita Cave Paintings&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Batanera Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; are notable examples, offering a glimpse into ancient history amidst your run. The traditional salt harvesting at the Salinas de Fuencaliente also represents a unique cultural practice.

Q: What is the best season for running in Fuencaliente?
A: Fuencaliente offers distinct microclimates, making it suitable for running year-round. The highlands of Los Canarios are cooler, while the coast enjoys a sunnier, drier climate. This variety allows you to choose paths based on your preferred conditions, though generally, the mild climate of La Palma makes it an excellent destination for outdoor activities throughout the year.

Q: Where can I park when accessing running trails in Fuencaliente?
A: Many running routes in Fuencaliente, especially those starting from villages like Solana del Pino or Fuencaliente itself, often have designated parking areas nearby. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304463084/sierra-de-navalmanzano-by-the-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita-circular-from-fuencaliente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Navalmanzano by the Pinturas Rupestres Peña Escrita — circular from Fuencaliente&lt;/a&gt; route starts directly from Fuencaliente, where parking options are typically available.

Q: What should I wear for running in Fuencaliente's varied terrain?
A: Given the varied terrain, including volcanic gravel (picón) and potential elevation changes, it's advisable to wear sturdy running shoes with good grip. Layered clothing is also recommended to adapt to the microclimates, as temperatures can vary between coastal areas and higher altitudes. Don't forget sun protection and sufficient hydration.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes in Fuencaliente?
A: Yes, Fuencaliente is home to several challenging running routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304426313/ascent-to-serra-madrona-circular-from-solana-del-pino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Serra Madrona — circular from Solana del Pino&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.9-mile (14.2 km) trail with substantial climbing. The famous GR 131 trail, also known as the Volcanic Route, crosses the island from Fuencaliente, offering a particularly challenging run.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Fuencaliente?
A: While many natural areas in La Palma are generally dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific trail regulations, especially within protected natural parks or reserves. When running with your dog, ensure they are on a leash, you carry waste bags, and they are comfortable with varied terrain, including volcanic gravel.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Fuencaliente?
A: For families, the easier and moderate trails are the best choice. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304449386/cascada-de-la-batanera-de-sierra-madrona-circular-por-las-pinturas-rupestres-pena-escrita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Batanera de Sierra Madrona — circular por las Pinturas rupestres Peña escrita&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option. Additionally, the easy 30-minute trail around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6110637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Fuencaliente&lt;/a&gt; offers a gentle path with unique scenery, suitable for a family outing.
tips: The Sierra de Navalmanzano is a massif today preserved in this border area between Ciudad Real and Jaén.

Schematic cave paintings. They are made up of zigzag triangular signs. It is a quartzite rock shelter that contains schematic cave paintings with zigzag triangular signs and human figures in a kind of ritual dance. They measure a total of 30 cm and to make them ocher and black colors were used.

Very close to the border with Andalusia, in Sierra Morena, and within the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park, is the municipality of Fuencaliente. It is famous for housing the cave paintings of Peñaescrito and La Batanera, declared a National Monument. It also owes its name to the thermal water spring that rises under the church of the Virgen de Baños, and that in its day attracted many pilgrims called by the healing power of its waters. This spring was declared a public utility asset in 1869 and continues to be a source of vitality. If we add to all this its unique urban ensemble, we find ourselves before a town worthy of the most beautiful tourist postcard.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this circular route is categorized as a difficult run. It involves significant elevation changes and varied terrain, making it more suitable for experienced joggers with good endurance.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse terrain, including rocky sections, a dirt track with many potholes, and 'stone beaches' along the Lastras del río Cereceda. The route also passes through forested areas with pines and oaks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant feature is the Peñaescrita Cave Paintings, ancient schematic rock art. You'll also pass through the Sierra de Navalmanzano, enjoy views of Fuencaliente village, and encounter natural features like the stunning Chorrera de los Batanes waterfall.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly scenic in autumn when the mountains display vibrant colors. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for jogging, avoiding the summer heat.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Fuencaliente. You should be able to find parking within the village to begin your run.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this trail or the Peñaescrita Cave Paintings.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, trails in natural parks like this one often allow dogs, typically on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations upon arrival in Fuencaliente to confirm specific rules for the area.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Fuencaliente, a village in Ciudad Real, Spain. Public transport options to smaller villages can be limited, so checking local bus schedules from larger towns in Ciudad Real is recommended, though driving is often the most convenient option.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a difficult jogging route like this?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate trail running shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a map or GPS device. Layers of clothing are also advisable for changing weather conditions.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino del Valle and the Camino de Fuencaliente a Solana del Pino.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants in Fuencaliente after the run?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fuencaliente is a village and likely offers local establishments where you can find refreshments and food after your run.
